'''Liam Harris''' (born 20 April 1997) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].

He played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] at the [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League 1|Kingstone Press League 1]]. He played for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Harris was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
In 2018 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Hull F.C.]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league |title=Hull KR 22-34 Hull FC|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

===Halifax===
On 17 November 2020, it was announced that Harris would join Halifax for 2021.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Hull Daily Mail|date=17 Nov 2020|accessdate=24 Dec 2020|title=Liam Harris leaves Hull FC after penning deal with Championship side|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/liam-harris-hull-fc-halifax-4710799}}</ref>

===York City===
On 12 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[York City Knights|York City]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=York snap up former Hull half-back Liam Harris|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-sign-hull-liam-harris-halifax/}}</ref>

On 7 June 2025, Harris scored all five points for York, including the match-winning [[Golden point#Rugby league|golden point]] drop goal, in their 5–4 win over [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the final of the [[2025 RFL 1895 Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm26dp2l3vvo |title=York pip Featherstone in extra time to win 1895 Cup |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=12 June 2025}}</ref>
